failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of the prosody definition (case-sensitive match bug)
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — normalize tag lookup to be case-insensitive in getTagDef()
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase, mixed-case, and case-insensitive isTagSupportedByProvider test cases
fixApproach: In getTagDef(), lowercase both the input tagName and each candidate t.tag before the equality check, so the comparison becomes case-insensitive. isTagSupportedByProvider() calls getTagDef() internally and benefits automatically. New tests cover uppercase, mixed-case, already-lowercase, nonexistent, and provider-support case-insensitivity paths.
steps: Modify getTagDef() in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts: normalize both tagName and t.tag to lower-case before comparing (e.g. const key = tagName.toLowerCase(); return tagDefs.find(t => t.tag.toLowerCase() === key))

Add test cases to apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts covering: uppercase lookup ('PROSODY'), mixed-case lookup ('Break'), already-lowercase lookup ('emphasis') returns correct def, nonexistent tag returns undefined, and isTagSupportedByProvider case-insensitivity
Run npm run test to verify all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive by lowercasing both sides of the tag name comparison
